live_position.market: CON +17.5 live spread / No - Las Vegas wins by over 17.5

model_assessment.failure_mode: Connecticut margin-compression thesis failed. Las Vegas paint dominance, offensive rebounding, second-chance scoring, and bench efficiency extended the lead beyond the live spread. Connecticut shooting regression never arrived.

live_entry_snapshot.entry_note: User locked CON +17.5 at +108 as a yellow micro-position based on FT survival, OREB support, possible shooting regression, and A'ja Wilson foul risk.

LVA.A_Wilson: 22 pts, 11 reb, 4 OREB, 2 blk, +33. Despite 4 PF, remained dominant and drove paint/rebound edge.
LVA.C_Carter: 27 pts, 8 reb, 4 ast off bench on 13-16 FG. Major bench spike and spread-extension driver.
LVA.C_Parker_Tyus: 10 pts, 5 reb, 3 OREB, 2 blk. Bench interior activity amplified second-chance edge.
LVA.C_Gray: 7 pts, 6 reb, 6 ast, +29. Stabilized offense and margin control.
LVA.J_Loyd: 11 pts off bench with 3 threes. Spacing support during extension phase.

CON.A_Morrow: 16 pts, 11 reb, 4 OREB but 4-16 FG. Rebound support existed, but efficiency did not regress enough.
CON.O_Nelson_Ododa: 14 pts, 6 reb on 5-7 FG. Efficient interior contributor but not enough volume.
CON.S_Rivers: 8 pts on 2-11 FG. Shot creation collapse.
CON.D_Miller: 5 pts on 1-9 FG. Perimeter inefficiency sustained.
CON.K_Burke: 4 pts, 5 ast, 4 TOV on 2-8 FG. Creation drag and turnover pressure.

rebound_environment.summary: Las Vegas dominated total rebounds 46-29 and offensive rebounds 12-8. LVA converted the glass into 22 second-chance points versus CON's 10, eliminating the expected Connecticut margin-compression path.
notes: The pre-entry CON OREB support existed early but did not sustain relative to LVA's interior dominance.
Aces produced 58 paint points and 22 second-chance points, creating a high-stability spread extension path.
Connecticut's 8 OREB and 20-24 FT were not enough because FG efficiency remained at 32.9%.
